text: Unit tests focus on individual components, like a single function or class, verifying they work correctly in isolation. Integration tests, on the other hand, check how different parts of the system interact with each other. Many developers follow test-driven development, where they write tests before writing the actual code. This approach can increase confidence significantly. When a project has a robust suite of tests, making changes becomes less risky. You can refactor or add new features, knowing that if you break something, the tests will quickly alert you. This safety net allows for faster development and higher-quality software. In short, good testing practices are essential for maintaining reliable code.
